danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy sprites appearing on overworld and dungeon maps
danger_signals.meaning: Active combat threat; monsters change by time of day/night cycles

danger_signals.visual_form: Boss-tier enemy sprites; documented example: Land Umber (Earth Benevodon)
danger_signals.meaning: Catastrophic danger to world; unleashing these entities destroys the world

danger_signals.visual_form: Character Kevin visually transforms into werewolf sprite at night
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental danger window; some enemies sleep during night but Kevin becomes uncontrolled threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemies displayed in dormant/sleeping animation state
danger_signals.meaning: Reduced immediate threat but hazard if awakened; time-dependent safety window

safety_signals.visual_form: Town structures with shelter visual designation
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone for rest; allows time-skipping to avoid dangerous night periods

safety_signals.visual_form: Stone monument sprites at multiple locations
safety_signals.meaning: Safe interaction points for character class advancement; neutral zones

safety_signals.visual_form: Bright overworld/dungeon lighting, diurnal enemy roster
safety_signals.meaning: Safer traversal period; fewer threats than night; Kevin remains human-form

safety_signals.visual_form: Clustered structures, NPC populations, distinct map zones
safety_signals.meaning: Social safe zones; areas of civilization vs. hostile wilderness

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Light-aligned class visual effects; holy magic spell effects

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Dark magic spell visual effects; shadowy sprite variants
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Nighttime dominance; certain locations/enemies tied to darkness

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Elemental Benevodon bosses (e.g., Land Umber for Earth element)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Massive boss-scale sprites; element-specific visual theming

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Each day tied to elemental identity; visual calendar representation
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Spell effectiveness varies by elemental spirit day
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Calendar/temporal markers affecting magic potency visually

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bright overworld lighting; full NPC activity in towns; Kevin maintains human form
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Most enemies active and hostile; standard combat difficulty; shops/services fully operational

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Darkened overworld/dungeon sprites; reduced lighting palette; Kevin transforms to werewolf sprite
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Different enemy roster appears; some creatures sleep; black markets open; Kevin attack power greatly increases but may become uncontrollable

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Calendar display showing current day and associated elemental spirit
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Magic of matching element gains power boost; enemy encounters shift by element
Base character skills and stat progressions only

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character sprite changes to reflect Light or Dark class choice; visual equipment/aura modifications
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: New skill sets unlock; different stat progression paths; altered special attack animations

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Second class change available (level 38+, with rare item)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mana Stone interaction available; second-tier class sprite variations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Advanced skill trees accessible; further specialization between Light and Dark paths

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss sprite display (e.g., Land Umber); catastrophic scale visual indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: World-ending threat gameplay; plot-critical boss encounters; three-character party coordinated combat

environmental_storytelling.example: Black market only appears at night; certain story events locked to specific times; enemy sleep states create narrative of temporal vulnerability
Kevin werewolf transformation at night communicates his curse/dual nature; visual transformation is immediate environmental storytelling without dialogue
Weekly cycle tied to elemental spirits communicates world's magical balance and natural rhythm; visual day-of-week markers show player the cosmic order
Mana Stones scattered across world geography signal path of character growth; locations hint at power sources and world's magical infrastructure

environmental_storytelling.technique: Enemy distribution by location and time
environmental_storytelling.example: Different enemy rosters in field and dungeon areas communicate ecological/faction territories; nocturnal vs. diurnal creatures establish environmental hierarchy

environmental_storytelling.technique: Light vs. Dark class visual dichotomy
environmental_storytelling.example: Player choice of Light or Dark class at level 18 visually manifests as sprite and aura changes; communicates moral/playstyle divergence without forcing plot consequences
Choice of 3 of 6 playable characters at start creates narrative branching; unchosen characters appear as NPCs, establishing alternative storylines and faction representation

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Awake/active (day or night, except sleeping enemies)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Pursue and attack player party; initiate combat sequences
Remain stationary; do not engage unless player initiates; communicates avoidance opportunity
Transforms to werewolf sprite; attack power greatly increases; suggests loss of control or primal strength awakening
Maintains human form; standard combat and movement behavior
Populate streets; operate shops; provide services and dialogue; communicate civilization and safety
Reduced presence; shops close except black market; communicate reduced social activity and danger
Massive sprite scale; world-threat combat; three-character party coordinated attack; communicate existential danger

notes: Seiken Densetsu 3 uses temporal mechanics (day/night cycles and weekly elemental calendar) as primary world-state communication method. Visual manifestations are minimal but functional: sprite changes for time of day, Kevin's werewolf transformation, and class-change sprite variations. The game communicates danger through enemy presence and type distribution rather than explicit visual warning systems. Faction identity relies on elemental/magical affiliation (Light vs. Dark classes) rather than color-coded armor or sigils. Environmental storytelling emphasizes accessibility gating (time-locked events, location-specific Mana Stones, NPC availability) over visual environmental design. The three-character party system enables multiple narrative paths without explicit faction visualization. World-state changes are primarily behavioral (enemy rosters, service availability, event triggers) rather than environmental texture/color shifts.
